description: Library of concrete objectives for the adab notebook, in English.

paroles_generiques.tenu: What is held in silence is kept by the One who sees the hidden. Keep the joy without weighing it.
paroles_generiques.parfois: To hold sometimes is already to hold. Constancy is born of honoured intermittence.
paroles_generiques.oublie: A fault acknowledged opens a door that innocence never knew. The return is already the path.
paroles_generiques.repris: To take it up smaller tomorrow is not a defeat — it is an act of wisdom.

matin.libelle: Listen without preparing my reply
matin.enseignement_court: To listen is to let the other exist without reducing him to my reply. It is a gift before it is a technique.
matin.phrase_chemin: You carry this presence to the other. Go.

soir.question: When was I tempted to cut in today? What was I missing?

paroles.tenu: Full listening is an act of adab often invisible to others — but not to the heart.
paroles.parfois: Complete attention is a path. You did your part.
paroles.oublie: Take it up smaller tomorrow: listen to one single person, fully, once.
paroles.repris: The return is already the path. Tomorrow, begin again.

matin.libelle: Not cut others off
matin.enseignement_court: To let the other finish is to grant him a dignity. To cut in is already to judge what one has not yet heard.
matin.phrase_chemin: Keep silence as a gift.

soir.question: How many times did I interrupt today? What pushed me to speak?

paroles.tenu: Keeping silence so the other may speak — that is already an act of adab.
paroles.parfois: Every silence kept is a fruit. Keep it without announcing it.
paroles.oublie: Ḥilm is learned through use. Tomorrow, wait half a second longer.
paroles.repris: Take it up smaller: one single conversation, tomorrow, without interrupting.

matin.libelle: Lower my voice before explaining
matin.enseignement_court: Truth spoken harshly often wounds the very one it meant to awaken. The low voice is an adab.
matin.phrase_chemin: A gentle voice opens where the loud voice closes.

soir.question: Did I raise my voice today to be right? What wanted to be heard?

paroles.tenu: A low voice carried by truth — that is the timbre of lovers.
paroles.parfois: Mastery of one's tone is a discipline. You have begun.
paroles.oublie: The loud voice often hides a fear. Spot it tomorrow before it comes out.
paroles.repris: Take it up in silence tomorrow: once, lower your voice before speaking.

matin.libelle: Not speak of one who is absent
matin.enseignement_court: To protect the absent is to keep an amāna of speech — a deposit entrusted to my silence.
matin.phrase_chemin: Silence about an absent one is an invisible protection.

soir.question: Did I comment on someone in their absence today? What did it say about me?

paroles.tenu: To keep the absent from gossip is to love him beyond the encounter.
paroles.parfois: A word withheld is more precious than a thousand words added.
paroles.oublie: To recognise ghība is already to begin undoing it. A silent blessing tomorrow erases a bad word from yesterday.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: choose one person, and keep silent about them, even if they come up in conversation.

matin.libelle: Say no without harshness
matin.enseignement_court: To say no without harshness is a faithfulness to truth. It is not closing the heart — it is recognising right measure.
matin.phrase_chemin: A just no protects the truth of the bond.

soir.question: Did I say yes today where my heart said no? What made me give in?

paroles.tenu: A no set down in gentleness is more precious than a forced yes.
paroles.parfois: Learning to say no is the work of a lifetime. You have begun.
paroles.oublie: The complacent yes is a lie to oneself. Spot tomorrow the moment you will give in.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: prepare a no, simple, without excuses, and hold it once.

matin.libelle: Hold back a useless word
matin.enseignement_court: Every word counts. What has no need to be said weighs down the inner silence.
matin.phrase_chemin: Silence is rarer than speech. Keep it.

soir.question: Did I speak to fill a void today? What should have been kept?

paroles.tenu: Chosen silence is a wordless praise.
paroles.parfois: The discipline of speech is built in flashes. You had your flashes.
paroles.oublie: To speak too much is often to flee an uncomfortable silence. Inhabit it tomorrow.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: choose one conversation where you will speak less than you listen.

matin.libelle: Avoid wounding irony
matin.enseignement_court: Irony protects the one who throws it and cuts the one who receives it. It is rarely the path of truth.
matin.phrase_chemin: Speak plainly, without arrows.

soir.question: Did I throw an irony today? What was it hiding?

paroles.tenu: Straight speech, without sting, is a rest for the other as for you.
paroles.parfois: Giving up irony is a gentle weaning. You are working at it.
paroles.oublie: Irony is an old armour. Spot it without judging yourself.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: spot one irony rising, and do not say it.

matin.libelle: Speak well of one who is absent
matin.enseignement_court: Where ghība tears, praise of an absent one repairs. What you say of him in his absence builds him.
matin.phrase_chemin: Bless him through speech.

soir.question: Did I speak well of someone today who was not there?

paroles.tenu: The silent praise of an absent one is a blessing that circulates.
paroles.parfois: Even a gentle word about someone counts.
paroles.oublie: We forget to bless those we love. Tomorrow, name one in good.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single person, one good word said in their absence.

matin.libelle: Say sorry explicitly
matin.enseignement_court: A pardon said clearly is worth a thousand implied ones. The word has its own power.
matin.phrase_chemin: Say the word. Not around it.

soir.question: Is there a pardon I dodged today by merely implying it?

paroles.tenu: To say sorry, simply, opens a door nothing else can open.
paroles.parfois: Even a stammered pardon works.
paroles.oublie: Pride holds back the word. Spot it without fighting it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one pardon, one word, to one single person.

matin.libelle: Listen to my child without correcting at once
matin.enseignement_court: A child who feels heard learns to trust himself. To correct too quickly is to teach him to hide.
matin.phrase_chemin: Listen to him as you would wish to be listened to.

soir.question: Did I let my child reach the end of what he had to say? What did I hear that was new?

paroles.tenu: A child listened to keeps the trace of that listening long after you have forgotten.
paroles.parfois: Every listening held counts. The child's heart counts them too.
paroles.oublie: Quick correction is an old reflex. Tomorrow, wait one more sentence before answering.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single moment, listen without stepping in. That is enough.

matin.libelle: Listen without correcting the other
matin.enseignement_court: To correct is already to step out of listening. Sometimes the other is not waiting for a correction — he is waiting to be heard.
matin.phrase_chemin: Let the other exist in his own truth.

soir.question: Did I pounce on a mistake to correct it? What would silence have brought instead?

paroles.tenu: To leave the other in his truth, even imperfect, is to love him.
paroles.parfois: The urge to correct often comes from fear. You named it sometimes.
paroles.oublie: The need to be right is a veil. Tomorrow, observe it without acting.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single conversation, with no correction at all.

matin.libelle: Listen while looking at the person
matin.enseignement_court: Listening passes through the gaze. To look away while listening is already to leave for elsewhere.
matin.phrase_chemin: Set down your gaze. Stay.

soir.question: Did I listen while looking today, or with my phone in my hands? Who deserved my whole gaze?

paroles.tenu: A gaze set upon the other is a silent gift.
paroles.parfois: The gaze is disciplined like speech. You have begun.
paroles.oublie: The phone is an easy veil. Tomorrow, set it down when someone speaks to you.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single conversation, without a screen, looking.

matin.libelle: Listen to someone angry without defending myself
matin.enseignement_court: Beneath anger there is often a wound. To listen to the wound rather than defend the form is to defuse.
matin.phrase_chemin: Listen, without defending yourself.

soir.question: Did someone speak to me harshly today? Did I listen, or return blow for blow?

paroles.tenu: To listen to anger without returning it is to disarm without wounding.
paroles.parfois: To hold partly before a storm is already a fruit.
paroles.oublie: The reflex of defence is old and deep. Honour it without following it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: if anger comes, wait three breaths before answering.

matin.libelle: Listen without offering a solution
matin.enseignement_court: We seek to solve in order to flee listening. Sometimes the awaited solution is presence.
matin.phrase_chemin: Stay. Without offering anything.

soir.question: Did I offer a solution where the other only wanted to be heard?

paroles.tenu: Listening without a solution is rarer and more precious than we think.
paroles.parfois: The reflex to solve calms through use.
paroles.oublie: The need to be useful can be a veil. Tomorrow, be useless once.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one conversation where you are not allowed to offer a solution.

matin.libelle: Listen to my spouse without answering at once
matin.enseignement_court: Those we love most often receive our least listening. One whole conversation changes the bond.
matin.phrase_chemin: Listen to him, or to her, like a precious stranger.

soir.question: Did I truly listen to my spouse today? Or did I only wait for my turn?

paroles.tenu: One full listening, to the one we love, is a seal.
paroles.parfois: The couple is built in repeated listenings.
paroles.oublie: Loved ones often receive our tiredness. Forgive yourself, and take it up again.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: a short moment, present, just to listen.

matin.libelle: Listen to the silence after the words
matin.enseignement_court: After a sentence, wait. The silence sometimes carries what the words did not say. Listening to silence is an art.
matin.phrase_chemin: Let the silence speak after the words.

soir.question: Did I listen to a silence today, or quickly fill every gap?

paroles.tenu: Silence listened to is a deeper listening than speech heard.
paroles.parfois: You are learning to inhabit the pauses.
paroles.oublie: Filling silences is a reflex of discomfort. Tomorrow, accept a pause.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one pause, three seconds, after someone has spoken.

matin.libelle: Set the phone down for five minutes
matin.enseignement_court: Five minutes without a screen is five minutes given back to what is. Presence is rarer than connection.
matin.phrase_chemin: Go, and leave the screen on the table.

soir.question: How many times did I reach for the screen without deciding to? What did I find when I left it?

paroles.tenu: Five inhabited minutes are worth an hour of empty scrolling.
paroles.parfois: Each pause taken is a silent victory.
paroles.oublie: The reflex is stronger than the decision — at first. Tomorrow, choose one moment of pause.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: a meal without a phone. Just once.

matin.libelle: Inhabit three breaths in the day
matin.enseignement_court: Three conscious breaths are enough to recall the heart to where it is. Presence needs only a second to return.
matin.phrase_chemin: Return to the breath. That is all.

soir.question: Did I recall myself to myself today? When?

paroles.tenu: Three inhabited breaths, and the day becomes a witness.
paroles.parfois: One single conscious breath is already a return.
paroles.oublie: The breath waits, without moving. Tomorrow, it will be there.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single breath, before the morning coffee.

matin.libelle: Eat slowly, one meal in presence
matin.enseignement_court: To eat is an encounter with what is given. To eat slowly is to honour the Giver.
matin.phrase_chemin: Eat what you eat.

soir.question: Did I taste what I ate today? Which meal passed without my seeing it?

paroles.tenu: An inhabited meal is a praise in silence.
paroles.parfois: One single meal eaten in presence reminds the body it is loved.
paroles.oublie: The hurried meal has become a habit. Tomorrow, choose one, and slow down.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single bite, eaten in presence, without a screen.

matin.libelle: Walk in presence for five minutes
matin.enseignement_court: To walk knowing one walks is already a prayer. The step becomes dhikr when the heart is there.
matin.phrase_chemin: Walk, and return to yourself as you walk.

soir.question: Did I walk today without running mentally? Where was I when my feet were here?

paroles.tenu: Five minutes of inhabited walking are worth a short retreat.
paroles.parfois: A conscious step is a step toward God.
paroles.oublie: The mind runs ahead of the feet. Tomorrow, slow down once.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one short journey, on foot, in presence.

matin.libelle: Prepare a meal in presence
matin.enseignement_court: To chop, to stir, to wait for the cooking — each gesture can be a dhikr if the heart stays there.
matin.phrase_chemin: Cook as one prays: without haste.

soir.question: Did I inhabit what I prepared today?

paroles.tenu: A meal prepared in presence nourishes twice.
paroles.parfois: A few inhabited minutes in the kitchen are already a fruit.
paroles.oublie: We often rush when preparing. Tomorrow, slow down one single time.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single dish, prepared without rushing.

matin.libelle: Pray one prayer in full presence
matin.enseignement_court: A distracted prayer passes; an inhabited prayer remains. Quantity does not save quality.
matin.phrase_chemin: One prayer, truly inhabited.

soir.question: Did I hold my presence in at least one prayer today?

paroles.tenu: One inhabited prayer is worth a thousand prayers full of distraction.
paroles.parfois: Presence in prayer is built prayer after prayer.
paroles.oublie: The mind is clever at fleeing. Return, gently, without judging yourself.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single rakʿa, in full presence. That is enough.

matin.libelle: Look at the sky for five minutes
matin.enseignement_court: To raise the eyes to the sky recalls the soul to its proportion. The sky asks nothing — it invites.
matin.phrase_chemin: Raise your eyes. Stay there.

soir.question: Did I raise my eyes to the sky today — really?

paroles.tenu: Five minutes of sky give the soul back its height.
paroles.parfois: A glance toward the sky is never lost.
paroles.oublie: The sky waits, patient. Tomorrow, it will be there.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single moment, eyes to the sky, without a phone.

matin.libelle: Wait somewhere without taking out the phone
matin.enseignement_court: Minutes of waiting are islands. To fill them with screen is to drown them. To inhabit them is to transform them.
matin.phrase_chemin: Wait by inhabiting the waiting.

soir.question: How many times did I take out the phone by reflex of waiting?

paroles.tenu: An inhabited wait becomes an encounter with oneself.
paroles.parfois: You are learning to bear the empty spaces.
paroles.oublie: The reflex to fill is strong. Spot it without fighting it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single queue, without a phone.

matin.libelle: Defer a difficult answer to tomorrow
matin.enseignement_court: A ripened answer is better than a true answer said too soon. Patience prepares rightness.
matin.phrase_chemin: Wait, without fleeing.

soir.question: Did I want to answer quickly today, where waiting would have served better?

paroles.tenu: To defer is not to retreat — it is to ripen.
paroles.parfois: Patience is discovered in crossing the delay.
paroles.oublie: Answering quickly soothes for an instant but costs for long. Tomorrow, delay an answer by an hour.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single time, say “I will answer you later.”

matin.libelle: Accept a delay without forcing it
matin.enseignement_court: To force time is to force God. Patience recognises that what must come will come in its hour.
matin.phrase_chemin: Entrust time to the One who holds it.

soir.question: Which delay annoyed me today? What was it trying to teach me?

paroles.tenu: The accepted delay becomes a school.
paroles.parfois: Patience before time is the work of a lifetime.
paroles.oublie: Impatience is a youth of the soul. It calms through use.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single delay, accept it without commenting on it.

matin.libelle: Wait five minutes before reacting
matin.enseignement_court: Five minutes between impulse and act often change the course of a day.
matin.phrase_chemin: Five minutes — and you will see differently.

soir.question: When the impulse came, did I wait? What should I have waited for?

paroles.tenu: Five minutes waited are a heart saved.
paroles.parfois: Waiting becomes a strength once you have tested it.
paroles.oublie: The impulse is a visitor — it leaves if you do not feed it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single impulse, wait five minutes before answering it.

matin.libelle: Not check the phone every five minutes
matin.enseignement_court: Compulsive checking comes from a fear of missing out. To miss out is also a grace.
matin.phrase_chemin: Set it down. Really.

soir.question: How many times did I check the phone needlessly today?

paroles.tenu: The hand that no longer reaches for the screen is a free hand.
paroles.parfois: To space out the checks is a weaning. You are working at it.
paroles.oublie: Compulsion can be tamed, not fought. Observe.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: set the phone in another room, for an hour.

matin.libelle: Wait for a reply without chasing it
matin.enseignement_court: To chase too quickly is to force the other. To wait is to trust him — and to trust time.
matin.phrase_chemin: Wait. The reply will come or will not come.

soir.question: Did I chase someone today who had not replied to me?

paroles.tenu: Not chasing is an act of gentle humility.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to respect others' time.
paroles.oublie: The need for a reply often reveals an insecurity. Honour it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single wait, without any chasing.

matin.libelle: Welcome the unforeseen without cursing it
matin.enseignement_court: The unforeseen is often the appointment God set without warning us. To curse it is to miss the appointment.
matin.phrase_chemin: The unforeseen may be your best teacher of the day.

soir.question: What unforeseen thing came today? How did I receive it?

paroles.tenu: An unforeseen thing welcomed is a door opened to grace.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to see beyond “this wasn't planned.”
paroles.oublie: Control is stubborn. Tomorrow, say “ahlan” to the unforeseen.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one unforeseen thing — welcome it without commenting.

matin.libelle: Bear the slowness of a loved one
matin.enseignement_court: Our pace is not the measure of the world. The other's slowness is not his flaw — it is his tempo.
matin.phrase_chemin: Slow down to join his step.

soir.question: Did I grumble inwardly at someone's slowness today?

paroles.tenu: To walk at a loved one's pace is to offer him your time.
paroles.parfois: You are learning to adjust your rhythm.
paroles.oublie: Visible impatience wounds. Invisible impatience too.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: follow a loved one at their pace, without shifting a gear.

matin.libelle: Ask forgiveness from someone I wounded
matin.enseignement_court: To ask forgiveness is not to collapse. It is to acknowledge. True tawba acts before it justifies.
matin.phrase_chemin: Return, simply. That is all.

soir.question: Did I find the words today? If not, what held me back?

paroles.tenu: To ask forgiveness opens a door no silent regret can open.
paroles.parfois: Even a start of a pardon opens something.
paroles.oublie: The delayed pardon remains possible. Tomorrow is still here.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one word, short, sincere. That is enough.

matin.libelle: Speak gently again to the one I was harsh with
matin.enseignement_court: To take up the same conversation, in a lower voice, is more powerful than a long speech.
matin.phrase_chemin: Speak again, as if the first time had not happened.

soir.question: Did I take up speech again with gentleness today? What did that gentleness allow?

paroles.tenu: A word taken up again in gentleness erases three harsh words.
paroles.parfois: To take it up again is already to love.
paroles.oublie: The reflex of defence is old. Tomorrow, observe it, without acting.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single sentence taken up again, gentler.

matin.libelle: Write without sending
matin.enseignement_court: To write what one will not say is already to say it to oneself. Not everything needs to leave to do its work.
matin.phrase_chemin: Write for yourself. Keep silence for the other.

soir.question: Did I write today what I had no need to send?

paroles.tenu: A letter kept is a word transformed.
paroles.parfois: Even a few lines written for oneself do their work.
paroles.oublie: The need to send is often a need to be heard. The journal already hears.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: three lines, for yourself alone.

matin.libelle: Keep a tiny forgotten promise
matin.enseignement_court: A tiny promise kept repairs more than a great promise remade.
matin.phrase_chemin: Do the small thing. It is the truth.

soir.question: Did I honour today a word given yesterday? Which one remains pending?

paroles.tenu: Keeping a tiny promise is the seal of character.
paroles.parfois: Even a part kept builds the heart.
paroles.oublie: A forgotten promise is not lost as long as it can be honoured tomorrow.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single promise, chosen, kept.

matin.libelle: Correct an unjust word said yesterday
matin.enseignement_court: An unjust word stands as long as one leaves it hanging. To restore it is to do justice to the one who was not there to defend himself.
matin.phrase_chemin: Set it right. Without drama.

soir.question: Is there an unjust word I left hanging today?

paroles.tenu: To restore the true is an act of quiet courage.
paroles.parfois: Even a stammered correction has its power.
paroles.oublie: We often prefer forgetting to setting right. Tomorrow, restore once.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one sentence, before the right people, to give back the truth.

matin.libelle: Bless someone I judged yesterday
matin.enseignement_court: To bless the one we judged is to undo the judgement from below. The blessing is stronger than resentment.
matin.phrase_chemin: May good be given to him.

soir.question: Did I bless in my heart someone I was judging?

paroles.tenu: The silent blessing undoes judgement at the root.
paroles.parfois: The heart learns to bless before judging.
paroles.oublie: Judgement will return. Bless it too.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one person, in your heart, in silence: “may good be given to him.”

matin.libelle: Reach someone I have drifted from
matin.enseignement_court: A short message to a loved one lost from sight mends more than a long accusing silence.
matin.phrase_chemin: One message, short, without excuses.

soir.question: Is there someone I could have reached today?

paroles.tenu: A thread retied is worth ten years of distance.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to break the silence.
paroles.oublie: Pride holds back. Tomorrow, spot whom you could reach.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single person, one single message, without expectation.

matin.libelle: Finish a small forgotten thing
matin.enseignement_court: Unfinished things weigh beyond their size. To finish one is to free a corner of the soul.
matin.phrase_chemin: Go finish that thing. That is all.

soir.question: Which small thing did I finally finish today?

paroles.tenu: A thing finished is a knot undone.
paroles.parfois: Even a step toward the end is a fruit.
paroles.oublie: Pending things are still waiting. Spot one.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one small thing, finished. One is enough.

matin.libelle: Thank a person sincerely
matin.enseignement_court: To recognise what another did for us is to make grace visible. Gratitude is lived in gestures before words.
matin.phrase_chemin: Say it. Simply. Today.

soir.question: Whom did I thank today? Who should have been thanked?

paroles.tenu: A sincere thank-you is a light that circulates.
paroles.parfois: Even a shy thank-you counts.
paroles.oublie: Silent gratitude becomes invisible. Tomorrow, say it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single person, one single thank-you.

matin.libelle: Name a protection received
matin.enseignement_court: To recognise a protection — an accident avoided, a word spared — is to make visible the hand that holds us.
matin.phrase_chemin: Look for the gift in what did not happen.

soir.question: What did not happen to me today that I should give thanks for?

paroles.tenu: To see the silent protections is the gaze of the knower.
paroles.parfois: Gratitude for what did not happen is rare. You have begun it.
paroles.oublie: Invisible protections await your gaze.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: before sleeping, name one thing that did not happen to you.

matin.libelle: Recognise a gift in the day
matin.enseignement_court: A gift received and recognised is a gift twice. A gift received and forgotten is a gift by half.
matin.phrase_chemin: Spot what is given to you. Without asking for it.

soir.question: What did I receive today that I had not asked for?

paroles.tenu: To recognise a gift is already to give thanks.
paroles.parfois: A gift glimpsed is worth more than ten gifts passed through.
paroles.oublie: The eye that does not see the gift withers. Tomorrow, look for one alone.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: before sleeping, name one gift of the day.

matin.libelle: Bless a meal before eating
matin.enseignement_court: A blessed meal is a meal that is seen. Bismillāh is enough — no need for a long formula, just a presence.
matin.phrase_chemin: Bless. Then eat.

soir.question: Did I pause an instant before a meal today?

paroles.tenu: Bismillāh said in presence turns bread into a gift.
paroles.parfois: A pause before a meal, and the heart recognises.
paroles.oublie: We often eat forgetting. Tomorrow, spot one meal to bless.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single meal, preceded by one single word.

matin.libelle: Thank my parents (living or not) in thought
matin.enseignement_court: The debt to one's parents does not die out. A thought turned toward them is a word heard, here or elsewhere.
matin.phrase_chemin: Turn toward them, in thought.

soir.question: Did I carry my parents in my heart today?

paroles.tenu: A thought for one's parents is heard, here or elsewhere.
paroles.parfois: The heart turns, in fits, toward those who made it.
paroles.oublie: We forget them. Tomorrow, one thought. Just one.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one prayer, short, for them.

matin.libelle: Not complain once today
matin.enseignement_court: Complaint is a veil over grace. Not to complain is not to repress — it is to shift one's gaze.
matin.phrase_chemin: When the complaint rises, wait. Find something else.

soir.question: Did I hold back a complaint today? What did that silence allow?

paroles.tenu: A heart that does not complain becomes a heart that sees.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to hear your own complaints before they come out.
paroles.oublie: Complaint is habitual. Do not fight it — observe.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: a complaint rising, wait three breaths before saying it.

matin.libelle: Name three graces of the day tonight
matin.enseignement_court: Before sleeping, three graces named by name. No more, no less. Three is a measure.
matin.phrase_chemin: Three graces. Before sleeping.

soir.question: Which three graces can I name for this day?

paroles.tenu: Three graces named open the eyes for tomorrow.
paroles.parfois: Even one grace named lights a lamp.
paroles.oublie: Sleep presses. Tomorrow, take a minute.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: three graces, written or thought, before bed.

matin.libelle: Bless inwardly a difficult person
matin.enseignement_court: To bless the other in silence is a discipline of the heart. It first unties the one who blesses.
matin.phrase_chemin: Say to him in your heart what you would not say aloud.

soir.question: Did I inwardly bless someone I found difficult today?

paroles.tenu: The heart that blesses is freed before the one who is blessed.
paroles.parfois: A blessing sketched is already a change.
paroles.oublie: Resentment is a familiar poison. Tomorrow, choose a person to bless.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: name a person, and say in your heart: “may good be given to him.”

matin.libelle: See a quality in someone I judge
matin.enseignement_court: Judgement reduces the other to an image. To see a quality undoes it.
matin.phrase_chemin: Look in the other for what your eye had not seen.

soir.question: What quality did I see today in someone I did not like to look at?

paroles.tenu: To see a quality in the other is to wash one's eyes.
paroles.parfois: A quality glimpsed in the other works in silence.
paroles.oublie: Judgement feeds closure. Tomorrow, spot a quality in one single person.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: choose someone, and find a quality. One is enough.

matin.libelle: Not feed a resentment
matin.enseignement_court: Resentment feeds on the scene one replays. To stop replaying is already to untie it.
matin.phrase_chemin: Let the scene fade. Do not relight it.

soir.question: How many times did I replay the same scene within me today?

paroles.tenu: A scene not replayed dies on its own.
paroles.parfois: Even an effort not to replay is a fruit.
paroles.oublie: Rumination is an old friend. Spot it with tenderness.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: when the scene returns, change activity once.

matin.libelle: Forgive someone inwardly
matin.enseignement_court: To forgive does not absolve the other. It frees you. Forgiveness is first for the one who gives it.
matin.phrase_chemin: Let go, for yourself.

soir.question: Is there someone I was able to forgive today — at least a little?

paroles.tenu: Inner forgiveness changes the heart of the one who forgives.
paroles.parfois: Forgiveness comes in layers. You have begun.
paroles.oublie: Forgiveness is slow. That is normal.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: spot what you still hold. Let go a little.

matin.libelle: Not compare myself today
matin.enseignement_court: To compare oneself is to want a destiny that is not one's own. Your path is traced for you, and no one else.
matin.phrase_chemin: Stay on your path. Without looking at others.

soir.question: How many times did I compare myself today?

paroles.tenu: A day without comparing is a day to oneself.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to notice comparison before it bites.
paroles.oublie: Comparison is clever. Return, gently.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: when comparison rises, tell yourself: “not my path.”

matin.libelle: Let someone be different from me
matin.enseignement_court: The other is not an unfinished version of me. His difference is his path, which need not pass through mine.
matin.phrase_chemin: Let him exist, in his own way.

soir.question: Did I try to change someone today rather than welcome them?

paroles.tenu: To let the other exist is a deep humility.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to see difference as a grace.
paroles.oublie: To want to change the other is a quiet pride. Spot it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: choose a person, and accept one thing about them, without saying anything.

matin.libelle: See a beauty in the day
matin.enseignement_court: Beauty does not wait to be sought — it waits to be seen. One single beauty recognised clothes the heart for the day.
matin.phrase_chemin: Look for a beauty. Recognise it.

soir.question: What beauty touched me today?

paroles.tenu: To see a beauty is an act of presence.
paroles.parfois: A beauty glimpsed leaves a trace.
paroles.oublie: We walk with lowered eyes. Tomorrow, raise them.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one beauty. Just one. Recognised.

matin.libelle: Accept one of my limits without fleeing it
matin.enseignement_court: Our limits are also our path. To deny them is to condemn oneself; to accept them is to begin to grow.
matin.phrase_chemin: Look at your limit. Without fleeing it.

soir.question: What limit did I meet today? Did I deny it or welcome it?

paroles.tenu: A limit accepted becomes a ground to lean on.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to honour what you cannot.
paroles.oublie: We flee our limits before seeing them. Return.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one limit — accept it, just once.

matin.libelle: A gesture of service without announcing it
matin.enseignement_court: An announced gesture loses half its fruit. A discreet gesture grows in silence.
matin.phrase_chemin: Do it, and say nothing of it.

soir.question: Did I do something for someone without saying it?

paroles.tenu: A discreet good is kept by the One who sees the hidden.
paroles.parfois: A gesture done, even small, is a fruit.
paroles.oublie: The urge to be seen is old. Tomorrow, do it and keep silent.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single gesture, without telling anyone.

matin.libelle: Ask someone for help
matin.enseignement_court: To ask is to recognise that I am not alone. The humility of asking is a door.
matin.phrase_chemin: Say it. You are not bound to carry everything.

soir.question: What did I carry alone today that could have been shared?

paroles.tenu: To ask is a gentle humility.
paroles.parfois: Even a start of a request changes the weight.
paroles.oublie: Self-reliance can be a closure. Tomorrow, ask once.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one request, short, clear, to one single person.

matin.libelle: Keep a small word given
matin.enseignement_court: Small words kept build a character. Great words without follow-through unmake it.
matin.phrase_chemin: Do the small thing. Today.

soir.question: Which small word did I honour today?

paroles.tenu: A tiny promise kept is worth a great promise unkept.
paroles.parfois: To hold sometimes builds trust with oneself.
paroles.oublie: A forgotten promise can be honoured tomorrow.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single small thing promised, done.

matin.libelle: Visit someone I neglect
matin.enseignement_court: A short visit to a forgotten one is worth a long prayer. The present in person is rare.
matin.phrase_chemin: Go. A short visit.

soir.question: Is there someone a visit would do good to — and whom I have forgotten?

paroles.tenu: A visit is a gift that memory does not replace.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to carry those who are far.
paroles.oublie: Time slips by. Tomorrow, spot a person to see.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: a call. A message. A meeting.

matin.libelle: Help someone without telling them
matin.enseignement_court: Announced help is also a gift, but hidden help is purer. It seeks no coin.
matin.phrase_chemin: Help. Without saying it.

soir.question: What help did I bring today without speaking of it?

paroles.tenu: Hidden help blesses first the one who helps.
paroles.parfois: Even a silent start counts.
paroles.oublie: The wish to be recognised is a veil. Spot it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single act of help, without mentioning it.

matin.libelle: Give something without anyone knowing
matin.enseignement_court: A hidden gift is a seal on the heart of the one who gives. The right hand does not know what the left hand does.
matin.phrase_chemin: Give. In secret.

soir.question: Did I give today — discreetly?

paroles.tenu: A hidden gift is more precious than ten exposed gifts.
paroles.parfois: The gesture counts, even small.
paroles.oublie: To give without appearing is rare. Return to it.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single gift, without anyone knowing.

matin.libelle: Listen to a loved one I avoid
matin.enseignement_court: The difficult loved one receives less presence — precisely because he is difficult. To give it to him is to undo an old knot.
matin.phrase_chemin: Give him a presence.

soir.question: Did I extend a little presence to a difficult loved one today?

paroles.tenu: A presence given to the one we avoid is a silent victory.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to see beyond “difficult.”
paroles.oublie: Avoidance protects, but isolates. Return.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: a short moment, a presence, to the one we avoid.

matin.libelle: Repeat a divine Name for five minutes
matin.enseignement_court: Five minutes of dhikr open a wake the day passes through. One single Name is enough, repeated in presence.
matin.phrase_chemin: Choose a Name. Inhabit it.

soir.question: Did I give time to dhikr today? Which Name accompanied me?

paroles.tenu: A Name repeated in presence works long after the lips have fallen silent.
paroles.parfois: Even a minute of dhikr is a seed.
paroles.oublie: Dhikr waits, faithful. Tomorrow, it will be there.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single Name, one single minute, in presence.

matin.libelle: Five minutes of silence before opening the phone
matin.enseignement_court: The first act of the morning orients the day. Silence before the screen returns the day to its beginning.
matin.phrase_chemin: Do not connect to them before connecting to Him.

soir.question: How did I begin the day today? What colonised my first minutes?

paroles.tenu: An inhabited beginning gives an inhabited day.
paroles.parfois: One single silent morning changes the week.
paroles.oublie: The screen before silence is a reflex. Tomorrow, delay it by five minutes.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: on waking, wait five minutes before turning on the screen.

matin.libelle: Read one page of a sufi text today
matin.enseignement_court: One page read slowly is worth ten pages devoured. The sufi text lets itself be infused, not swallowed.
matin.phrase_chemin: Read one page. Close it. Let it work.

soir.question: Which word of a master accompanied me today?

paroles.tenu: A page read in presence is a companion for days.
paroles.parfois: Even a few lines infuse.
paroles.oublie: The text waits, patient. Tomorrow.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one page only. Read slowly.

matin.libelle: Repeat a Name 99 times (subḥa)
matin.enseignement_court: 99 repetitions of a Name, on the subḥa or on the fingers, carve a furrow in the heart. Repetition is not empty — it is polishing.
matin.phrase_chemin: 99 times. Slowly.

soir.question: Did I hold my 99? How did I inhabit this repetition?

paroles.tenu: 99 Names carried in presence transform the day behind them.
paroles.parfois: Even 30 inhabited repetitions are a fruit.
paroles.oublie: The subḥa waits in the pocket. Tomorrow.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: 33 times is enough. But in presence.

matin.libelle: Read a short sura in presence
matin.enseignement_court: A short sura — al-Fātiḥa, al-Ikhlāṣ, al-Falaq — read with the heart, opens a space in the day.
matin.phrase_chemin: One sura. Inhabited.

soir.question: Which sura did I read today — truly read?

paroles.tenu: A short sura read in presence is worth a long distracted recitation.
paroles.parfois: Even a few inhabited verses infuse.
paroles.oublie: The text waits, open. Tomorrow.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: al-Fātiḥa, once, in presence.

matin.libelle: Meditate one single verse
matin.enseignement_court: One verse carried all day long works more than a sura recited ten times. Choose one. Inhabit it.
matin.phrase_chemin: One verse. All day long.

soir.question: Which verse accompanied me today?

paroles.tenu: A verse inhabited becomes a companion.
paroles.parfois: Even a few returns to the verse count.
paroles.oublie: The verse waits, without moving. Tomorrow.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one short verse. Keep it close to the heart.

matin.libelle: Say astaghfirullāh 100 times
matin.enseignement_court: To ask forgiveness is not to weep over a fault — it is to turn the heart toward the source. 100 astaghfirullāh undo 100 veils.
matin.phrase_chemin: Turn the heart. 100 times.

soir.question: Did I set down my astaghfirullāh today?

paroles.tenu: 100 returns are 100 doors opened.
paroles.parfois: Even 30 astaghfirullāh work.
paroles.oublie: The asking for forgiveness waits. Tomorrow, without drama.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: 33 times. With presence.

matin.libelle: Breathe before each answer
matin.enseignement_court: A breath before the answer changes the quality of the answer. The breath slows the mind.
matin.phrase_chemin: Breathe in. Then speak. Not before.

soir.question: How many answers did I give today without breathing first?

paroles.tenu: A breath between question and answer is a hidden adab.
paroles.parfois: The breath is quicker to forget than speech. You return to it.
paroles.oublie: The mind runs. The breath waits, patient.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single answer, preceded by a breath.

matin.libelle: Recognise my fatigue before it speaks for me
matin.enseignement_court: Unrecognised fatigue dictates decisions one will regret. To name it is already to undo it by half.
matin.phrase_chemin: If you are tired, slow down. Before it speaks.

soir.question: Did my fatigue speak for me today?

paroles.tenu: To recognise one's fatigue is a humility of the body.
paroles.parfois: Even a moment of recognition changes what follows.
paroles.oublie: Denied fatigue bites. Tomorrow, listen to it sooner.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: at the first feeling of fatigue, pause for five minutes.

matin.libelle: Not make an important decision at day's end
matin.enseignement_court: The evening is a poor counsellor. To postpone a decision to tomorrow morning is often a wisdom.
matin.phrase_chemin: Postpone. Tomorrow morning, you will see differently.

soir.question: Did a decision come this evening that would have served better tomorrow morning?

paroles.tenu: To postpone wisely is also an act.
paroles.parfois: Even an accepted delay changes the outcome.
paroles.oublie: The evening presses, but is wrong. Tomorrow morning will be here.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: if a decision arrives this evening, tell yourself: “tomorrow morning.” Once.

matin.libelle: Rest truly, without a screen
matin.enseignement_court: Rest with a screen is not rest. The body asks for a true silence.
matin.phrase_chemin: Rest. With nothing.

soir.question: Did I truly rest today, or did I fill my pauses?

paroles.tenu: A true rest is an act of faith.
paroles.parfois: One silent pause is worth ten noisy pauses.
paroles.oublie: The reflex to fill is a flight. Tomorrow, leave it empty.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: one single pause, with nothing, five minutes.

matin.libelle: Go to bed early tonight
matin.enseignement_court: To sleep early is not a premature old age — it is to honour the body. Sleep is a gift; to push it away is to despise it.
matin.phrase_chemin: Set things down. Go to sleep.

soir.question: Will I sleep early tonight, or push it back again?

paroles.tenu: An early bedtime is a practical wisdom.
paroles.parfois: You are beginning to respect your sleep.
paroles.oublie: The screen keeps you awake. Tomorrow, set it down earlier.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: thirty minutes earlier than yesterday. That is enough.

matin.libelle: Drink water regularly today
matin.enseignement_court: The body asks for water more often than we think. To give it is an act of presence to oneself.
matin.phrase_chemin: Drink. Before being thirsty.

soir.question: How many times did I listen to thirst today?

paroles.tenu: A hydrated body is a body listened to.
paroles.parfois: The body receives what you give it.
paroles.oublie: We forget the simple. Tomorrow, a glass on waking.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: a glass of water on waking. Just once.

matin.libelle: Walk a few minutes after a meal
matin.enseignement_court: To walk after the meal wakes the body. Five minutes are enough. The body receives the walk like a caress.
matin.phrase_chemin: Get up. Walk a little.

soir.question: Did I walk after a meal today?

paroles.tenu: A short walk after a meal honours the body.
paroles.parfois: Even a few steps count.
paroles.oublie: We collapse. Tomorrow, get up.
paroles.repris: Tomorrow: after a meal, five steps. At the least.
